engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz G Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The OM642 is the most common G Class diesel engine, powering over 70% of UK-market diesel G Class models from 2006 to 2023.
compatibleVariants: G 320 CDI, G 350 CDI, G 350 CDI Edition, G 350 d, G 350 d Professional, G 350 BlueTEC
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal/filter housing leaks (affects ~80% of OM642 engines over 60k miles)
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss (TSB 07-3105)
Turbocharger carrier bolt failure causing oil/coolant leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel (Long Stroke Variant)
entries.history: A later evolution of the OM642 platform with revised turbocharger mounting, fitted primarily to post-2015 G Class models with enhanced emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: G 350 d (2015-2023), G 350 d AMG Line, G 350 d Professional
commonFailures: Turbocharger carrier bolt failure (P/N A 642 096 11 45 required for repair)
DPF regeneration failure on urban-driven vehicles
AdBlue system crystallisation
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 LS Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes’ modular inline-6 diesel introduced with the W463A generation G Class, featuring CAMTRONIC variable valve lift and stepped bowl combustion technology.
compatibleVariants: G 350 d (W463A), G 400 d, G 400 d AMG Line, G 450 d
commonFailures: Coolant pump seal failure
CAMTRONIC actuator issues (A6560500001)
High-pressure fuel system sensitivity
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM656 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel (Naturally Aspirated)
entries.history: The legendary OM603 inline-6 diesel that defined G Class durability in the early W463 generation. Known globally for extreme longevity but requires precise injection system maintenance.
commonFailures: Injector pump calibration drift (A6030700001)
Glow plug relay failure affecting cold start
Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM603 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel (Turbocharged)
entries.history: The high-performance turbodiesel that transformed the G Class driving experience. The OM606.964 produced 177 hp and remains highly sought after for restoration projects.
compatibleVariants: G 300 TD, G 300 Turbodiesel, G 300 Turbodiesel Cabriolet (463.308)
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch (requires replacement at 150k+ miles)
Injector pump calibration failure (A6030700001)
Turbocharger bearing wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM606 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The original G Class diesel that built the model's reputation for indestructibility. Fitted to military and civilian W460/W461 variants across the globe.
compatibleVariants: G 300 GD (W460/W461), G 300 GD Military, 240 GD (OM616 2.4L variant also available)
commonFailures: Head gasket failure (common across all military/civilian variants)
Pre-chamber seal leaks
Injector pump seal deterioration
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM617 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.familyHeading: M176 / M177 / M178 V8 Turbo Family (2015-present)
entries.history: The hot-V turbo V8 that replaced the M278 in 2015. Features cylinder deactivation and twin-scroll turbochargers positioned inside the V-angle for reduced lag.
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold cracking (A1761400001)
High-pressure fuel pump failure (A0010739301)
Oil separator/PCV system failure (A1760180001) causing consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for M176 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (AMG)
entries.familyHeading: M176 / M177 / M178 V8 Turbo Family (2015-present)
entries.history: The high-performance AMG version of the hot-V V8, fitted to G 63 models from 2018. Uses wet-sump lubrication and delivers up to 585 hp.
compatibleVariants: G 63 AMG (W463A), G 63 AMG Edition 463, G 63 AMG Grand Edition
commonFailures: Top timing chain tensioner wear (requires precision calibration)
Intercooler efficiency degradation on tuned vehicles
Turbocharger wastegate corrosion
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.5L Petrol
entries.history: The hand-built AMG V8 that powered the first-generation G 63. Each engine was signed by its AMG assembler, but known for specific turbo-related failures.
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure (A1570940001) causing power loss
Valve guide/seal installation defect (documented on low-mileage AMG units)
Intercooler cooling performance degradation
entries.cta: Get quotes for M157 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: The durable 5.0L V8 that defined G 500 ownership for over a decade. Simpler than later turbo units but susceptible to timing chain tensioner wear across all displacements.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (shared component A1130500232 fails identically across all M113 displacements)
Map thermostat failure causing overheating and slow warm-up
Valve guide wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.4L Petrol (Kompressor)
entries.history: The supercharged 5.4L V8 that powered G 55 AMG. The M113K added a Lysholm-type supercharger to the robust M113 block, creating instant torque delivery.
compatibleVariants: G 55 AMG, G 55 AMG Kompressor, G 55 AMG Magna
commonFailures: Supercharger clutch bearing failure
Intercooler pump failure
Timing chain tensioner wear (shared with M113)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113K Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: The smooth 3.2L V6 that offered a more accessible petrol G Class entry point. Shared architecture with the M113 V8 but with two fewer cylinders.
commonFailures: Balance shaft gear wear (pre-2004 models)
Camshaft position sensor failure
Oil separator clogging
entries.cta: Get quotes for M112 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.6L Petrol
entries.history: The original G Class V8 that brought luxury performance to the model line. Shared architecture with contemporary S-Class (R107/SEC) platforms.
commonFailures: Valve guide wear (shared with R107/SEC chassis)
Fuel distributor failure on K-Jetronic systems
Timing chain stretch
entries.cta: Get quotes for M117 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure - Why G Class Diesel Engines Leak Oil Excessively
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 320 CDI, G 350 CDI, G 350 d (2006-2023, OM642 DE30 LA & OM642 LS)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil cooler seals (A6421800003 revised part), flush cooling system, new oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles if using revised Viton seals

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace complete oil cooler unit with revised seals, coolant flush, thermostat inspection
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; recommended if cooler shows corrosion

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt OM642 with upgraded seals, new oil pump chain, turbo inspected

problems.recommendation: If caught early with only external weeping, the seal-only specialist fix at £900-£1,400 is excellent value and should last 60k+ miles with revised seals. However, if the leak has caused coolant contamination or the engine has overheated, we recommend the full rebuilt route - contaminated oil galleries often cause turbo or bearing failure within 10k-15k miles of a partial repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz G Class

problems.h4: M157 / M177 Turbo Actuator Failure - Why AMG G Class Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 63 AMG (2012-2018, M157), G 63 AMG (2018-present, M177)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty turbo actuator, recalibrate wastegate position, road test
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-70k miles; viable if turbo core is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both actuators (twin turbo), inspect turbo cores, new gaskets
Supply & fit rebuilt M157/M177 with upgraded actuators, new turbos if required
repairOptions.longevity: Full long-block warranty; for catastrophic turbo debris ingestion

problems.recommendation: On an AMG G Class worth £35,000-£80,000, specialist actuator replacement at £600-£950 is the obvious first step. Dealer pricing is inflated for this repair. However, if the failure has caused turbo debris to enter the engine (metal particles in oil), you must go straight to full engine replacement - ignoring this risks complete bottom end failure within 1,000 miles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M157 or M177 Mercedes Benz G Class AMG engine replacement

problems.group: OM603 / OM606 Timing Chain & Injector Pump Failure
problems.h4: OM603 / OM606 Timing Chain & Injector Pump Failure - Classic G Class Diesel Problems
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 300 GD (1990-1993, OM603), G 300 Turbodiesel (1996-2000, OM606)

repairOptions.dealerPrice: Not available (dealers won't part-repair classic diesels)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; no pump work
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles; chain-only if pump still calibrated

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector pump removal, bench calibration, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-70k miles; for running issues without chain noise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt OM603/OM606 with new chain, rebuilt pump, upgraded tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles; corrects both failure points simultaneously

problems.recommendation: On a classic G Class worth £15,000-£40,000, we recommend chain replacement and pump calibration together as a preventative investment. Doing one without the other often leaves the owner chasing problems within 12-18 months. Main dealers are rarely interested in these classics - use a specialist who knows OM60x engines intimately.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM603 and OM606 engine prices for classic Mercedes Benz G Class

problems.group: M113 / M113K Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.h4: M113 / M113K Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - G Class V8 Rattle on Start-Up
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 500 (1998-2008, M113), G 55 AMG (2004-2012, M113K)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner only, oil and filter change
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-40k miles; only viable if no chain damage yet

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both chains, all tensioners, guides, sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; corrects the root cause properly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M113/M113K with upgraded chain components
repairOptions.longevity: Full long-block warranty; required if chain has jumped

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start rattle, do the tensioner-only specialist fix (£250-£400). If the rattle lasts more than 2 seconds or you've ignored it for 10k+ miles, go straight to the full chain set. On supercharged M113K engines (G 55 AMG), we are more aggressive - the higher torque loads accelerate chain wear, so full set replacement is usually the safer answer.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M113 or M113K Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

problems.h4: M176 / M177 Exhaust Manifold Cracking - Turbocharged G Class Petrol Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 500 (2015-present, M176), G 63 AMG (2018-present, M177)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both exhaust manifolds, new gaskets, turbo seals inspected
Replace manifolds with revised castings, service both turbos, new oil/coolant lines
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; recommended for tuned or high-mileage cars

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M176/M177 with revised manifolds, upgraded turbo components
repairOptions.longevity: Full long-block warranty; for cracked block or debris damage

problems.recommendation: On a G 500 or G 63 worth £40,000-£100,000+, specialist manifold replacement with revised castings is essential. The dealer parts are the same weak design unless a later revision exists. If you've driven with a cracked manifold for months, hot debris may have damaged turbo seals - budget for the upgraded manifolds plus turbo service route at £3,500-£5,000 rather than a partial repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M176 or M177 Mercedes Benz G Class engine prices

problems.h4: OM656 CAMTRONIC & AdBlue System Failures - Modern G Class Diesel Complexity
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz G Class G 400 d, G 450 d (2019-present, OM656 D29)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CAMTRONIC actuator, recalibrate valve lift adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; viable if caught before cam damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace AdBlue dosing module, flush system, software update
Supply & fit rebuilt OM656 with updated CAMTRONIC components, AdBlue system reset
repairOptions.longevity: Full long-block warranty; required if actuator failure damaged cams

problems.recommendation: If your G 400 d throws a CAMTRONIC fault, address it immediately - continued driving can damage camshafts and lifters, turning a £700-£1,100 repair into a £6,000+ engine replacement. The AdBlue issues are irritating but not engine-threatening; however, a specialist £500-£800 fix is far better value than dealer pricing. These modern diesels reward preventative attention.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM656 Mercedes Benz G Class engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz G Class
finalCta.paragraph: A failed engine doesn't have to mean the end of your G Class. Whether you're dealing with an OM642 oil leak, an AMG turbo actuator failure, or a classic OM603 timing chain issue, EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz G Class specialists. Every rebuilt engine in our network includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point, a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery with supply & fit options available.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz G Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for your Mercedes Benz G Class is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Factory-spec engine strip-down, inspection and component replacement using OEM or OEM-quality parts. Returns engine to measured tolerances.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec engine strip-down, inspection and component replacement using OEM or OEM-quality parts. Returns engine to measured tolerances.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best balance of cost and long-term durability for most G Class owners.
types.backDescription: Industry-recognised "recon" process strips every component to the bare block. Failed parts (pistons, rings, bearings, seals) are replaced with OEM-spec items.
backBullets: OM642/M176/M177 codes fully warrantied for 24 months
Ideal for G Class owners keeping their vehicle 3+ years
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Mercedes Benz G Class engine prices

types.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Engine disassembled, inspected and fitted with new piston rings, bearings, gaskets and seals. Worn parts replaced as needed.
types.frontDescription: Engine disassembled, inspected and fitted with new piston rings, bearings, gaskets and seals. Worn parts replaced as needed.
types.frontDisclaimer: Quality varies by specialist - use only EngineMarket-vetted suppliers.
types.backDescription: A "rebuild" focuses on bringing the engine back to reliable service without full remanufacturing standards. Excellent value for older or higher-mileage G Class models.
backBullets: Shorter turnaround than recon at 5-10 working days
All OM603, OM606 and M113 classics covered

types.frontDisclaimer: Fastest option but highest risk - verify mileage and compression reports.
types.backDescription: Budget-conscious choice for G Class owners with tight timelines. Typically removed from rear-end or side-impact donor vehicles with verifiable mileage and service history.

Compression test reports available on request

types.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Remanufactured Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Premium choice - closest you can get to a brand-new Mercedes Benz engine.
types.backDescription: The gold standard for G Class preservation. Each reman unit is stripped to bare metal, ultrasonically cleaned, and reassembled with 100% new critical components.
backBullets: All OM656, M177 and M279 units hot-tested before shipping
Unlimited mileage warranty up to 36 months
Includes new timing chains, oil pumps and turbochargers

types.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cosmetic and mechanical "tidy-up" of a used engine. Gaskets, seals and external components replaced - internal rotating parts untouched.
types.frontDescription: Cosmetic and mechanical "tidy-up" of a used engine. Gaskets, seals and external components replaced - internal rotating parts untouched.
types.frontDisclaimer: Entry-level option - understand the limitations before purchase.
types.backDescription: Refurbished sits between used and rebuilt. The engine receives new external seals, a fresh cambelt/chain where accessible, and a thorough clean for visual presentation.
backBullets: 1-2 day turnaround for common G Class codes

Suitable for low-mileage G 350 d with external leaks only

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz G Class across all generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 2.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.9-litre inline-six diesel arrived with the W463A generation G Class in 2019, replacing the long-serving OM642 V6. Codenamed OM656, this all-alloy unit introduced CAMTRONIC variable valve lift on the exhaust side and a two-stage turbocharger system producing exceptional 700Nm torque delivery from just 1,200rpm . Available initially as the G 400 d, the 2.9L later expanded to G 450 d variants, offering the most powerful diesel ever fitted to a G Class with 243kW output while maintaining refinement that eliminates traditional diesel clatter .
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue dosing module crystallisation on urban-driven vehicles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz G Class 2.9L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 diesel has been the backbone of G Class diesel sales since 2006. Mercedes-Benz introduced the OM642 as a 72-degree V6 replacing the straight-five and straight-six OM600 series engines, featuring common-rail direct injection at 1,600 bar and aluminium block/head construction . The OM642 powered the dominant G 320 CDI, G 350 CDI, and later G 350 d variants across both W463 and early W463A generations. A long-stroke OM642 LS variant appeared from 2015 with revised turbo mounting and emissions hardware. This engine family represents approximately 70% of UK G Class diesel replacement enquiries, largely due to well-documented seal failures at higher mileages.
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler seal/filter housing leaks (~80% affected over 60k miles)
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz G Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 4.0L Petrol Engines
commonFailurePoints: oil separator/PCV valve failure causing consumption
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.0-litre M113 V8 powered the G 500 throughout the W463 classic era from 1998 to 2008. This naturally aspirated SOHC 24-valve V8 produced 296 hp and built a reputation for robust durability, though the single-row timing chain and hydraulic tensioner require attention at higher mileages. The M113 was later supercharged to create the M113K for G 55 AMG models, adding a Lysholm-type supercharger to deliver 476 hp from 2004 to 2012. Both variants share the same M113 block architecture and many common wear components.
commonFailurePoints: valve guide wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class 5.0L and 5.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.5-litre M157 V8 biturbo powered the first-generation G 63 AMG from 2012 to 2018. This hand-built AMG engine produced 544 hp and was signed by its individual assembler - a hallmark of AMG's "One Man, One Engine" philosophy. The M157 featured twin turbochargers, direct injection, and a 90-degree V-angle in an aluminium block. While delivering explosive performance, these units have developed a reputation for specific turbo-related failures that drive replacement demand on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo actuator failure (A1570940001) causing limp mode
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class 5.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.0-litre M279 V12 biturbo represents the absolute pinnacle of G Class engine offerings, fitted exclusively to the ultra-rare G 65 AMG from 2015 to 2018. This hand-assembled 48-valve V12 produced 630 hp and 1,000 Nm of torque, delivering effortless performance that defies the G Class's boxy aerodynamics . With fewer than 500 units produced globally and UK examples exceptionally rare, the G 65 remains a collector's piece. Replacement engines command premium pricing due to low production numbers and V12-specific components. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz G Class needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz G Class specialists.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class 6.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.tag: Mercedes Benz G Class Engines Fuel Type
fuelTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engines by Fuel Type

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of UK G Class sales since the W463 generation, diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries - particularly the OM642 V6 which powers approximately 70% of diesel models on British roads.
items.descriptor: The backbone of UK G Class sales since the W463 generation, diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries - particularly the OM642 V6 which powers approximately 70% of diesel models on British roads.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz G Class Diesel Engine Families
families: OM642 - 3.0L V6, the most searched replacement engine (G 320 CDI, G 350 CDI, G 350 d), oil cooler seal failure risk
OM656 - 3.0L inline-6, current-generation diesel (G 400 d, G 450 d), CAMTRONIC actuator and AdBlue issues
OM603 - 3.0L naturally aspirated inline-6, classic G 300 GD, legendary durability but injector pump calibration drift
OM606 - 3.0L turbodiesel inline-6, G 300 Turbodiesel (1996-2000), highly sought after for restoration projects
OM617 - 3.0L inline-5, original G Class diesel (W460/W461), military-grade simplicity but head gasket failure common
foundIn: W463 Early (1990-2006) - OM603 (G 300 GD), OM606 (G 300 Turbodiesel)
W463 Facelift (2006-2018) - OM642 (G 320 CDI, G 350 CDI, G 350 d)
W463A (2018-present) - OM642 (early G 350 d), OM656 (G 400 d, G 450 d)
knownFor: Exceptional low-range torque suited to G Class off-road capability
OM642 oil cooler seal failure affecting ~80% of units over 60k miles
High replacement demand on G 350 d (most common UK fleet variant)
OM656 CAMTRONIC and AdBlue crystallisation issues on urban-driven vehicles
OM603/OM606 timing chain stretch requiring preventative replacement at 120k-150k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz G Class Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Petrol Engines
items.description: From the durable M113 V8 to the fire-breathing M177 biturbo, G Class petrol engines span three decades of Mercedes performance engineering - with AMG variants commanding premium replacement costs.
items.descriptor: From the durable M113 V8 to the fire-breathing M177 biturbo, G Class petrol engines span three decades of Mercedes performance engineering - with AMG variants commanding premium replacement costs.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz G Class Petrol Engine Families
families: M176 - 4.0L biturbo V8 (G 500 2015-present), cylinder deactivation, exhaust manifold cracking risk
M177 - 4.0L biturbo V8 AMG (G 63 AMG 2018-present), hand-built, 585 hp, top timing chain wear
M157 - 5.5L biturbo V8 AMG (G 63 AMG 2012-2018), turbo actuator failure common
M113/M113K - 5.0L/5.4L V8 (G 500 1998-2008, G 55 AMG 2004-2012), supercharger clutch failure on Kompressor
M112 - 3.2L V6 (G 320 1998-2005), smoother entry-level petrol, balance shaft gear wear
M279 - 6.0L biturbo V12 (G 65 AMG 2015-2018), ultra-rare, 630 hp, turbo bearing wear
knownFor: AMG variants represent highest-value replacement enquiries (£7,500-£25,000)
M113 timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle on 80k-120k mile units
M157/M177 hot-V design concentrates heat, leading to manifold cracking
Supercharged M113K requires specialist rebuild knowledge for Kompressor unit
G 65 AMG V12 has extremely limited parts availability and specialist network
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz G Class Petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz G Class Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The G 450 d introduced a 48-volt mild hybrid system to the diesel range in 2024, adding electric assistance without plug-in capability.
items.descriptor: The G 450 d introduced a 48-volt mild hybrid system to the diesel range in 2024, adding electric assistance without plug-in capability.
items.familiesLabel: Mercedes Benz G Class MHEV Engine Codes
families: OM656 - 3.0L inline-6 diesel with integrated starter-generator (ISG)
Provides up to 20 hp electric boost under acceleration
Enables smoother stop-start and coasting at low speeds
foundIn: G 450 d (2024-present) with OM656 diesel engine
importantNotes: 48-volt system adds complexity but does not affect engine replacement pricing significantly
Battery health check recommended during engine swap
No plug-in hybrid or full EV G Class exists for UK market (EQG expected future)
Specialist knowledge required for MHEV system disconnection/reconnection

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 1979 W460 military-spec original to the 2024 electrified W465 generation, this timeline covers every major engine fitted to the Mercedes-Benz G-Class - helping you identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The G-Class (Geländewagen) launched as a military-inspired off-roader with modest inline engines - a far cry from today's AMG monsters.
keyChanges: W460 launch with short-wheelbase (SWB) cabrio, SWB station wagon, long-wheelbase (LWB) station wagon body styles
230 G (2.3L petrol) and 240 GD (2.4L diesel) as initial UK options
Military-spec W461 continued alongside civilian W460 from mid-1980s
Solid axles, ladder-frame chassis and three differential locks established
mainEngines: M115 - 2.3L petrol (230 G) - most common early petrol
OM617 - 3.0L diesel (300 GD) - five-cylinder, legendary durability
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz 230 G · Mercedes-Benz 240 GD · Mercedes-Benz 300 GD
knownFor: Built for military durability, not luxury or speed
OM617 diesel regularly exceeds 300,000 miles with basic maintenance
Slow, noisy, but virtually unbreakable
300 GD remains highly sought after for classic restoration projects
years.cta: Get quotes for 1979-1989 Mercedes-Benz G-Class replacement engines

years.description: The W463 generation arrived in 1990 with updated interiors, ABS brakes, and electronic diff locks - plus the legendary OM603 diesel.
keyChanges: New W463 chassis with improved interior comfort
ABS brakes and electronic differential lock controls
OM603 3.0L inline-six diesel replaced OM617 five-cylinder
Petrol range updated with M103 inline-six engines
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz 280 GE · Mercedes-Benz 300 GE · Mercedes-Benz 300 GD
knownFor: OM603 injector pump calibration drift requires specialist attention
More comfortable on-road than W460 but still distinctly utilitarian
Electronic diff locks easier to use than mechanical predecessor
300 GD remains a favourite for overlanding builds
years.cta: Get quotes for 1990-1992 Mercedes-Benz G-Class replacement engines

years.description: The first V8-powered G-Class arrived in 1993 as a limited edition of just 500 units - the spiritual ancestor of every AMG G-wagen that followed.
keyChanges: 5.0L M117 V8 introduced as limited-run 500 GE (500 units)
Automatic transmission only on V8 model
Four-speed 722.3 auto gearbox from Mercedes sedan range
AMG offered a 6.0L conversion producing 326 hp
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz 500 GE · Mercedes-Benz 300 GE · Mercedes-Benz 300 GD
knownFor: Ultra-rare UK availability - most examples remain in Germany or Japan
Front diff lock was controversially deleted on 500 GE
AMG 6.0L conversion produces 525 Nm - extraordinary for 1993
Values for surviving 500 GE have appreciated significantly
years.cta: Enquire about 1993 Mercedes-Benz G-Class engine availability (limited edition)

years.description: The OM606 turbodiesel replaced the naturally aspirated OM603, delivering 177 hp and transforming the G-Class into a genuine high-speed cruiser.
keyChanges: OM606 3.0L turbodiesel introduced (G 300 Turbodiesel)
4-valve-per-cylinder head for improved breathing
Intercooled turbocharger for reduced EGTs
G 320 petrol switched from inline-six to M112 V6
mainEngines: OM603 - 3.0L diesel (G 300 GD) - continued for base models
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 300 Turbodiesel · Mercedes-Benz G 320 · Mercedes-Benz G 300 GD
knownFor: OM606 is one of Mercedes' most over-engineered diesel engines
Easily tuned to 250-300 hp with pump modifications
Timing chain stretch requires replacement at 120k-150k miles
Highly sought after for classic restorations and conversions
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-1997 Mercedes-Benz G-Class engine replacement

years.description: 1998 marked the permanent return of the V8 petrol engine to the G-Class range with the modern M113 5.0L, and the G 500 became the flagship model.
keyChanges: G 500 launched with M113 5.0L V8 - 296 hp
V8 available across all body styles (SWB, LWB, cabrio)
5-speed automatic transmission (722.6) replaced 4-speed
G 300 Turbodiesel continued as diesel flagship
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 320 · Mercedes-Benz G 300 Turbodiesel
knownFor: G 500 defined the modern G-Class luxury off-roader formula
M113 V8 timing chain tensioner wear at 80k-120k miles
G 500 remains one of the most popular classic G-Class variants
Cabrio versions from this era command significant premiums

years.description: Mercedes introduced the OM628 V8 diesel in 2001 - a 4.0L common-rail unit producing 250 hp, briefly making the G 400 CDI the most powerful diesel G-Class available.
keyChanges: G 400 CDI launched with OM628 4.0L V8 diesel - 250 hp
G 270 CDI introduced as 2.7L 5-cylinder diesel base model
7G-TRONIC 7-speed automatic introduced on select variants
Electronic traction control improvements
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 400 CDI · Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 270 CDI · Mercedes-Benz G 300 Turbodiesel
knownFor: OM628 V8 diesel is rare - only 4,000 units produced globally
G 400 CDI provides exceptional torque (560 Nm) for towing
G 270 CDI (156 hp) was the most fuel-efficient G-Class of its era
V8 diesel parts availability is now challenging
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Mercedes-Benz G-Class replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes-Benz G 400 CDI engine replacement • Mercedes-Benz G 500 V8 rebuild cost • OM628 diesel parts availability UK

years.description: 2005 saw the first AMG-branded G-Class officially join the range - the G 55 AMG Kompressor with a supercharged 5.4L V8 producing 476 hp, later increased to 500 hp.
keyChanges: G 55 AMG Kompressor launched as standard catalogue model (previously special order)
M113K 5.4L supercharged V8 - 476 hp (500 hp from 2006)
G 320 CDI launched with OM642 3.0L V6 diesel - 224 hp
G 270 CDI and G 400 CDI phased out as OM642 replaced both
mainEngines: M113K - 5.4L supercharged V8 petrol (G 55 AMG) - 476-500 hp
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 55 AMG Kompressor · Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 320 CDI
knownFor: G 55 AMG established AMG as a permanent G-Class fixture
Supercharger clutch bearing failure is a known weak point
M113K produces 700 Nm - enormous torque for mid-2000s
OM642 3.0L V6 diesel would dominate G-Class sales for 15+ years

years.ticker: G 55 AMG supercharger repair • OM642 engine replacement cost • G 55 Kompressor rebuilt engine price

years.description: The 2008 facelift brought a larger 5.5L M273 V8 to the G 500 (388 hp) and G 500 horsepower increased to 388 hp, while the G 55 AMG remained the performance flagship.
keyChanges: G 500 upgraded from M113 5.0L to M273 5.5L V8 - 388 hp
G 55 AMG power increased to 500 hp (unchanged from 2006 update)
Bi-xenon headlamps and LED DRLs introduced
OM642 V6 diesel continued in G 320 CDI
mainEngines: M113K - 5.4L supercharged V8 petrol (G 55 AMG) - 500 hp
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 55 AMG · Mercedes-Benz G 320 CDI
knownFor: M273 engine has better fuel economy than M113 but similar timing chain issues
G 500 0-60 mph dropped to 5.9 seconds
G 55 AMG Kompressor production continued until 2011 in some markets
OM642 oil cooler seal issues begin to appear on higher-mileage examples

years.description: The 2012 facelift introduced the M157 twin-turbo V8 in the G 63 AMG (544 hp), replacing the supercharged G 55 AMG - and the legendary G 65 AMG V12 arrived in 2015.
keyChanges: G 63 AMG launched with M157 5.5L twin-turbo V8 - 544 hp
G 350 BlueTEC introduced with updated OM642 (211 hp, Euro 6)
G 65 AMG launched (2015) with M279 6.0L twin-turbo V12 - 612 hp
ECO start-stop system introduced on AMG models
mainEngines: M157 - 5.5L twin-turbo V8 petrol (G 63 AMG) - 544 hp
M279 - 6.0L twin-turbo V12 petrol (G 65 AMG) - 612 hp
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 63 AMG · Mercedes-Benz G 65 AMG · Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 350 BlueTEC
knownFor: M157 turbo actuator failure (A1570940001) is a common high-mileage issue
G 65 AMG is ultra-rare (fewer than 500 units globally) with stratospheric values
G 350 BlueTEC Euro 6 compliance crucial for ULEZ city driving
OM642 oil cooler leaks remain the most common diesel failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2015 Mercedes-Benz G-Class replacement engines
years.ticker: G 63 AMG turbo actuator replacement • G 350 BlueTEC OM642 engine cost • G 65 AMG engine availability

years.description: The final years of the original W463 chassis saw continued refinement of the M157 AMG V8 and OM642 diesel range, with no major mechanical changes.
keyChanges: G 63 AMG M157 remains at 544 hp
G 350 d replaced G 350 BlueTEC branding
G 500 continued with M273 5.5L V8
Last chance to buy the original boxy W463 before 2018 redesign
mainEngines: M157 - 5.5L twin-turbo V8 petrol (G 63 AMG) - 544 hp
M279 - 6.0L twin-turbo V12 petrol (G 65 AMG) - 630 hp (2015-2018)
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz G 63 AMG · Mercedes-Benz G 65 AMG · Mercedes-Benz G 500 · Mercedes-Benz G 350 d
knownFor: Final year of the M157 G 63 AMG - many consider it the last "true" AMG G-wagen
G 350 d became the best-selling UK diesel variant
Values for low-mileage W463 G 65 AMG now exceed £200,000
W463 classic body style ended after 28 years of production

years.description: The first complete G-Class redesign in 39 years - the W464 launched with new chassis, 4.0L V8 engines, and vastly improved on-road refinement while retaining the classic boxy look.
keyChanges: All-new W464 platform with independent front suspension
G 500 upgraded to M176 4.0L twin-turbo V8 - 422 hp
G 63 AMG upgraded to M177 4.0L twin-turbo V8 - 585 hp
G 350 d continued with OM642 V6 diesel (but phased out in some markets)
G 400 d launched with OM656 3.0L inline-6 diesel - 330 hp
9G-TRONIC 9-speed automatic replaces 7G-TRONIC

OM656 CAMTRONIC actuator and AdBlue crystallisation issues

W464 transformed on-road comfort without losing off-road capability

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz G Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mercedes G Class engine in the UK?
items.answer: A Mercedes G Class engine replacement typically costs between £4,500 and £15,000 depending on engine code, condition type, and whether fitting is included. OM642 diesels start around £4,800, while G63 AMG units range from £8,500 to £15,000.
keyPoints: Reconditioned OM642 (G350d): £4,800-£7,500 supply only
Rebuilt M157 (G63 AMG 2012-2018): £7,500-£12,000 supply only
Used engines from breakers: £2,000-£5,500 but higher risk
Labour for supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 to any engine price
items.cta: Compare Mercedes G Class engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the OM642 diesel engine in the G Class reliable?
items.answer: The OM642 is generally robust but has a known weak point - the oil cooler seals fail on ~80% of units after 60,000 miles, causing oil and coolant to mix. Address this preventatively and the engine easily exceeds 150,000 miles.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seal failure is the most common G350d issue
Turbocharger carrier bolts can fail, causing oil leaks
EGR cooler cracking affects earlier OM642 variants
With revised seals, 150k+ miles is realistic
items.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes G Class replacement engines

items.question: What is the most common G63 AMG engine problem?
items.answer: The M157 (2012-2018) and M177 (2018-present) turbo actuators fail between 45,000-70,000 miles, causing limp mode and power loss. The M157 actuator (A1570940001) is the most frequent AMG G-Class failure driving replacement enquiries.
keyPoints: Turbo actuator failure triggers limp mode and check engine light
Exhaust manifold cracking on hot-V M177/M176 engines
M157 valve guide wear documented on low-mileage AMG units
Top timing chain tensioner wear on M177 G63 models
items.cta: Find reconditioned M157 or M177 Mercedes G Class engines

items.question: Which G Class engine should I avoid when buying used?
items.answer: No engine is fundamentally flawed, but high-mileage M157 (pre-2015) without documented turbo actuator replacement carries risk, and pre-2015 OM642 without oil cooler seal history needs immediate attention. Post-2018 OM656 diesel is the most reliable modern choice.
keyPoints: Early M157 (2012-2014) has highest actuator failure rate
OM642 needs oil cooler seals done every 60k-80k miles
OM656 (G400d) has fewer reported failures than OM642
M113 V8 (pre-2008) is simple and durable but thirsty
items.cta: Compare Mercedes G Class engine prices by variant

items.question: How long does a Mercedes G Class engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained G Class diesel engine regularly reaches 200,000-300,000 miles, with OM603/OM606 classics exceeding 300k. Petrol V8s typically last 150,000-200,000 miles before needing major work. The chassis itself can outlast multiple engines.
keyPoints: OM606 turbodiesel (1996-2001) is legendary for 300k+ mile longevity
OM642 needs oil cooler seals every 60k-80k to reach 200k
M113 V8s commonly reach 150k-200k with timing chain attention
Modern OM656 (G400d) too new for longevity data but promising
items.cta: Get quotes for long-lasting rebuilt Mercedes G Class engines

items.question: Should I repair or replace my G Class engine?
items.answer: Repair if the failure is isolated (e.g., oil cooler seals, turbo actuator, timing chain tensioner). Replace if there's bottom end damage, bore scoring, or multiple major failures. For G Classes worth £25,000+, a specialist rebuilt engine often makes more financial sense than chasing partial repairs.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seal replacement at specialist: £900-£1,400 - excellent value
M157 turbo actuator replacement: £600-£950 - do this first
Full OM642 rebuild: £4,800-£7,500 - for catastrophic failure or 150k+ miles
G63 AMG full rebuild: £8,500-£15,000 - still less than new vehicle depreciation
items.cta: Compare Mercedes G Class engine repair vs replacement costs

items.question: What is the most fuel-efficient G Class engine?
items.answer: The OM656 3.0L straight-six diesel in the G400d returns approximately 11.9L/100km (24 mpg UK) in mixed driving - remarkably efficient for a 2.5-tonne brick-shaped SUV. The G63 AMG V8 averages just 13-14 mpg.
keyPoints: G400d (OM656) returns ~24 mpg with 700Nm torque
G350d (OM642) returns ~22-23 mpg
G500 V8 petrol returns ~15-18 mpg
G63 AMG V8 returns ~13-14 mpg
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes G Class diesel replacement engines

items.question: How do I know which engine code my G Class has?
items.answer: Check your vehicle log book (V5C) for the engine number, which includes the engine code prefix (e.g., OM642, M176). Alternatively, enter your registration number in our tool above - we pull exact engine data from DVLA records instantly.
keyPoints: V5C log book shows full engine number with code prefix
Engine code stamped on cylinder block (visible from under vehicle)
Service history documents list engine type
Our DVLA tool identifies engine in 10 seconds
items.cta: Find your Mercedes G Class engine code and compare prices

items.question: Can I fit a used G Class engine myself?
items.answer: We strongly advise against DIY fitting on a G Class. The complex 4MATIC system, gearbox alignment, and coding requirements demand specialist equipment and expertise. A poorly fitted engine can destroy the transfer case or cause electrical failures costing more than professional fitting.
keyPoints: 4MATIC transfer case alignment requires specialist jigs
ECU coding and immobiliser synchronisation needed
Engine and gearbox must be separated correctly
Specialist supply & fit costs £800-£1,500 - worth every penny
items.cta: Find Mercedes G Class engine supply & fit deals from accredited specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned G Class engine?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with many specialists offering 24-month coverage on OM642 and AMG rebuilds. This includes parts and labour for any manufacturing defect or installation error.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage is the minimum standard
OM642 and AMG rebuilds often include 24-month warranty
All warranties cover parts, labour, and MOT compliance
Extended warranties available at point of purchase
items.cta: Compare Mercedes G Class engines with 24-month warranty coverage
